name: Sonora (Mexico)

colors.symbolism: the field and details of the arms
the desert sun, mineral wealth and details of the arms
the Sonoran Desert and details of the arms

description: A white field with the coat of arms of the state of Sonora centred.
history: Sonora has no officially adopted state flag; the design shown — a white field bearing the state coat of arms — is in de facto use to represent the state.

features.symbolism: A white field charged with the state coat of arms, in the typical pattern of Mexican state flags.
The arms reference the Yaqui deer dancer and indigenous heritage of Sonora, the agricultural, mining, livestock and fishing traditions of the state, and its place within the Mexican federation.
